summary: PR #2562 matched two stage functions, `grGreatBay_801F60C4` and `grInishie1_801FC4A0`, touching Great Bay and Inishie stage source files. The bot report recorded 2 new matches and +1016 matched-code bytes.

key_files.role: Refined `grGreatBay_801F60C4` source shape to reach a full match.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists `main/melee/gr/grgreatbay` `grGreatBay_801F60C4` +31 bytes, 94.67% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Extended a stage data struct and implemented `grInishie1_801FC4A0`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grInishie1_stuff adds unk44/unk48/unk4C/unk50 and implements grInishie1_801FC4A0
text_corpus: bot report lists `main/melee/gr/grinishie1` `grInishie1_801FC4A0` +452 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%

what_changed.change: `grGreatBay_801F60C4` removed the separate `damage` local and mutates/uses `arg3` directly after the landing-rate adjustment.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `s32 damage = arg3;` removed; landing-rate multiplication assigned to `arg3`

what_changed.change: `Vec3 pos` was moved into the non-null JObj block, and JObj assignment was folded into the null check.
Negative clamp branches were simplified by comparing directly against negated Great Bay limits instead of introducing temporary `min` locals.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removal of `f32 min = -..._limit` blocks for slant, slide, and down limits

what_changed.change: `grInishie1_stuff` gained four trailing float fields named `unk44`, `unk48`, `unk4C`, and `unk50`.

`grInishie1_801FC4A0` was implemented to decay `gp->gv.inishie1.xF0` toward zero using `grI1_804D69F8->unk50`, reset several movement/state fields when zero is reached, and update two JObj Y translations with `HSD_JObjSetTranslateY`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: full added body of grInishie1_801FC4A0

smart_moves.text: Kept unknown stage struct and GroundVars fields conservatively named (`unk50`, `xF0`, `x108`, `x10C`) rather than inventing semantic names not proven by the PR evidence.
Used existing typed stage and JObj access paths (`gp->gv.greatbay3`, `gp->gv.inishie1`, `HSD_JObjSetTranslateY`) instead of raw pointer arithmetic.
Included a narrow verification command in the PR body and received a bot report showing both target functions reached 100%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report with 2 new matches

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small stage-function matches, local variable lifetime and declaration placement can be match-relevant: `Vec3 pos` moved into the JObj-present block while other locals were split around it.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGreatBay_801F60C4 moves `Vec3 pos` from function top to inside `if ((jobj = ... ) != NULL)`

decomp_lessons.lesson: Directly reusing a parameter after an adjustment can match better than preserving a semantic temporary copy; this PR replaced `damage` with adjusted `arg3` in `grGreatBay_801F60C4`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `damage` local removed and `arg3` used in subsequent floatfloor calculations
text_corpus: bot report shows grGreatBay_801F60C4 reached 100%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Clamp-like code should be tested in direct comparison/assignment forms as well as with temporary min/max locals; the direct negated-limit form was part of the matched Great Bay cleanup here.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removal of temporary `min` locals and direct comparisons to `-..._limit`

naming_conventions.convention: Retain address-style function names and unknown field names when the PR evidence does not prove a semantic name.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grInishie1_801FC4A0 implementation and grInishie1_stuff fields

naming_conventions.convention: Use short local role names where roles are clear from type and access pattern.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Preserved a narrow `PAD_STACK(4)` in `grGreatBay_801F60C4` while adjusting local declarations and expressions around it.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows grGreatBay_801F60C4 reached 100%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR does not explain why `PAD_STACK(4)` is required; retain as PR-local evidence, not a global style rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Folded assignment into condition for the JObj null check: `if ((jobj = gp->gv.greatbay3.jobj) != NULL)`.
Introduced an explicit `zero` float local and `reached` integer flag in `grInishie1_801FC4A0` to express sign-dependent convergence to zero.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added `s32 reached = 0; f32 zero = 0.0f;` and sign branches

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used existing JObj setter inline/helper calls rather than expanded transform writes for Y translation updates.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 showed matched code 69.37% (+0.03%, +1016 bytes) and 2 new matches: `grInishie1_801FC4A0` and `grGreatBay_801F60C4`.
No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: grgreatbay floatfloor slant slide down limit direct clamp
stage function matching local declaration placement Vec3 pos

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR number/title/author/state/merge timestamps and changed-file counts
raw_diff: complete diff for two changed files
text_corpus: bot issue comment with match report
notes: The slice contains no human review comments, so reviewer intent or requested changes cannot be inferred.
The PR body reports `python configure.py --require-protos && ninja`; the bot report provides match percentages and bytes, but the slice does not include a separate objdiff transcript.
The `PAD_STACK(4)` use is visible and associated with a matched function, but the PR does not document why it is necessary.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Local declaration placement helped match grGreatBay_801F60C4
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2562, `grGreatBay_801F60C4` reached 100% after local-shape cleanup including moving `Vec3 pos` inside the non-null JObj block and splitting float declarations near use.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows grGreatBay_801F60C4 94.67% to 100.00%

accepted_candidate_records.title: Conservative unknown names were retained for matched Inishie stage code
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2562 implemented `grInishie1_801FC4A0` while retaining unknown field names such as `unk50`, `xF0`, `x108`, and `x10C`, matching the conservative-naming standard when semantics were not proven in the PR evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grInishie1_stuff fields and grInishie1_801FC4A0 body
text_corpus: bot report shows grInishie1_801FC4A0 0.00% to 100.00%

rejection_notes: Do not promote `PAD_STACK(4)` as a general tactic from this PR alone; the slice shows it remained in a matched function but does not explain necessity or adjacent-regression checks.
Do not infer semantic names for `grInishie1_stuff` trailing fields or GroundVars fields from this PR; the diff intentionally uses unknown/address-style naming.
Do not claim human reviewer approval details beyond merge status; no human reviews or review comments are loaded.
